base_read._comment: Seeded v2 (no discovery). Assigned-payroll base rows for the hard-coded seeded assignments E15/E16/E6, correlated to the person/assignment via pay_pay_relationships_dn (payroll_term_id = relationship-level term, id just above payroll_relationship_id; the lagging pay_rel_groups_dn is deliberately avoided). Scoped to the seeded Biweekly payroll id 300000051084930 and THIS run's prefix-derived start_date (${PAY_DATE_DASH}), so any row it returns is unambiguously this run's assigned-payroll segment.
base_read.sql: SELECT a.assignment_number AS ASG_NUM, MAX(ap.assigned_payroll_id) AS ASSIGNED_PAYROLL_ID, MAX(ap.payroll_id) AS PAYROLL_ID FROM pay_assigned_payrolls_dn ap JOIN pay_pay_relationships_dn pr ON pr.legislative_data_group_id=300000046974970 AND ap.payroll_term_id BETWEEN pr.payroll_relationship_id AND pr.payroll_relationship_id + 5 JOIN per_all_assignments_m a ON a.person_id=pr.person_id AND a.effective_latest_change='Y' AND a.assignment_type='E' AND a.primary_flag='Y' WHERE ap.legislative_data_group_id=300000046974970 AND ap.payroll_id=300000051084930 AND ap.start_date=DATE '${PAY_DATE_DASH}' AND a.assignment_number IN ('E15','E16','E6') GROUP BY a.assignment_number
